perturbation.original_text: The following definitions and interpretations shall apply to certain terms used in this Agreement: (a) Affiliate: A company is the Affiliate of another if either company owns directly or indirectly less than 50% but no less than 10% of its equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The following definitions and interpretations shall apply to certain terms used in this Agreement: (a) Affiliate: A company is the Associate of another if either company owns directly or indirectly less than 20% but no less than 5% of its equity.
perturbation.explanation: The term **'Affiliate'** has been replaced with **'Associate'** and the ownership percentage is altered. This contradicts the original definition later in the document, particularly in sections dealing with transfer of capacity to Affiliates based on the original definition of 10% - 50% ownership. This creates ambiguity as the parties are not aware about the revised or original definition being referred. The definition has been changed to 5% - 20%
perturbation.contradicted_text: Transfer of a Party's Allocated Capacity to its Affiliate and/or the Affiliate of the Party's Parent Company is also allowed provided that the capacity transferred is in multiples of the MIU and that the recipient of the transferred capacity is a Carrier. For the Transfer to an Affiliate, the transferring party shall make a cash contribution to a special fund dedicated solely to fund future upgrades to increase the Equipped Capacity. The amount of the contribution is described in Annex 7.
perturbation.justification: The modified text redefines 'Affiliate' as 'Associate' and alters the ownership percentage from 50% >10% to 20%> 5%. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which allows transfer of capacity to an 'Affiliate' based on the original definition. The change creates ambiguity about which definition of affiliate applies, making it unclear whether transfer to entities with 5-9% or 21-49% ownership is permissible.

perturbation.original_text: Capacity of APCN 2 can only be used by a carrier.
perturbation.changed_text: Capacity of APCN 2 can only be used by a service provider.
perturbation.explanation: The original text enforces **capacity use by a 'carrier'**, whereas the modified version replaces **'carrier'** with **'service provider'**. It is not clearly defined in this section if both are interchangeable, and contradicts what has been originally defined. This creates potential confusion as a 'service provider' can be interpreted to be different from a 'carrier'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A Party is allowed to use its WAC for the provision of international telecommunications services with itself, its Subsidiary, its Parent Company and/or the Subsidiary of the Party's Parent Company provided that the correspondent parties are also Carriers.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'carrier' with 'service provider' in the initial clause, whereas the contradicted clause explicitly requires 'correspondent parties' to also be 'Carriers'. If a 'service provider' is not explicitly defined as a 'carrier' or vice-versa, this creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted clause allows usage only if the other party is a carrier, which might not be the case under the new definition.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Management Committee decides to go to arbitration in accordance with Paragraph 27, each Party specifically reserves, and is granted by each of the other Parties, in any action, arbitration or other proceeding between or among the Parties or any of them in a country other than that Party's own country, the right of privileges, in accordance with the laws of the country in which the arbitration or litigation takes place with respect to any documents or communications which are material and pertinent to the subject matter of the action, arbitration or proceeding in which privilege could be claimed or asserted by that Party in accordance with those laws.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Management Committee decides to go to mediation in accordance with Paragraph 27, each Party specifically reserves, and is granted by each of the other Parties, in any action, mediation or other proceeding between or among the Parties or any of them in a country other than that Party's own country, the right of privileges, in accordance with the laws of the country in which the mediation or litigation takes place with respect to any documents or communications which are material and pertinent to the subject matter of the action, mediation or proceeding in which privilege could be claimed or asserted by that Party in accordance with those laws.
perturbation.explanation: The term **'arbitration'** has been replaced with **'mediation'**. The terms are not the same and introduce confusion. Paragraph 27 refers to the original method of dispute resolution as arbitration while this paragraph has now been changed to mediation. Due to this confusion about the method, there is a contradiction about which method is to be used, resulting in confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If a dispute should arise under this Agreement between or among the Parties they shall make every reasonable effort to resolve such dispute. However, in the event that they are unable to resolve such dispute, the matter shall be referred to the Management Committee which shall either resolve the matter or determine the method, such as arbitration, by which the matter should be resolved. This procedure shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any dispute which may arise under this Agreement between or among the Parties. The performance of this Agreement by the Parties shall continue during the resolution of any dispute.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'arbitration' to 'mediation' in the Changed Text introduces a direct contradiction with the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text states that the Management Committee will determine the method of dispute resolution, 'such as arbitration'. The Changed Text replaces 'arbitration' with 'mediation', causing a conflict regarding which method is actually to be used if the Management Committee does not resolve the matter itself. This misaligned terminology creates ambiguity and uncertainty, resulting in an in-text contradiction according to the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.
